fail_to_pass.name: dynamic_base_url_types_exposed
fail_to_pass.description: The public `BetterAuthOptions` type for `baseURL` accepts BOTH a static string (the existing shape) AND a dynamic config object. The dynamic shape includes at minimum `allowedHosts: string[]` (required), and supports optional `fallback?: string` and optional `protocol?: "http" | "https" | "auto"`. The dynamic object's type is named (e.g. `DynamicBaseURLConfig` or any equivalent) and re-exported from the public `@better-auth/core` types so application code can refer to it without reaching into internal modules. Both branches of the `baseURL` union are part of the option's static type — applications can assign either shape without a TypeScript error.

fail_to_pass.name: per_request_isolation
fail_to_pass.description: When the `baseURL` is configured as the dynamic-config object, `auth.handler` resolves the per-request origin into a per-request context derivation (e.g. `Object.create(ctx)`, an object spread, a `structuredClone`, or any equivalent clone strategy) rather than mutating the shared `AuthContext` for fields that vary per-request. Concurrent requests for distinct allowed hosts must not contaminate each other's resolved baseURL, cookie domain, or trustedOrigins. A handler that assigns `ctx.options.baseURL = ...` directly on the shared context for the dynamic-config path FAILS this criterion — that's the race-prone naive shape the issue specifically calls out.

fail_to_pass.name: consumer_type_safety
fail_to_pass.description: Every pre-existing consumer of `options.baseURL` that previously assumed a string (cookies/createCookieGetter, jwt sign/verify, mcp plugin, oauth-proxy plugin, oidc-provider plugin, passkey routes, telemetry, svelte-kit isAuthPath, test-instance client baseURL computation) safely handles the wider type after the option's static type is widened. Acceptable shapes include (a) explicit `typeof options.baseURL === "string" ? options.baseURL : <fallback>` narrowing, (b) routing the value through a resolver helper that handles both shapes, or (c) any other narrowing that prevents the dynamic-config object from being stringify-coerced into JWT issuer / passkey rpID / cookie domain / telemetry project ID values. The workspace-wide TypeScript build (`pnpm typecheck`) must remain green.
